div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister Resident in Saudi Arabia (Moose)
div.p:
130. It has been agreed by the Department and the Foreign Office that the American and British Governments send the following instruction
mutatis mutandis to their respective diplomatic representatives at Jidda, these instructions being repeated to Mr. Landis’ office in Cairo by the DepartmentRepeated to Cairo as telegram 1781, “for AEMME” (American Economic Mission, Middle East, Cairo headquarters of James M. Landis). and to Lord MoyneBritish Minister Resident in the Middle East. by the Foreign Office:
“The joint programme for supply and financial aid towards Saudi Arabia which has been agreed upon by the U.S. Government and His Majesty’s Government in the United Kingdom is to be regarded as the expression of a single combined policy in this field towards Saudi Arabia on the part of the two Governments. In the light of this policy you are to treat all proposals for supply and financial aid to Saudi Arabia jointly with your British colleague. You should cooperate with him closely and wholeheartedly. Any approach to the Saudi Arabia Government in this field should in future be made jointly with him.
2. Identical instructions are being sent to your British colleague.”
